titles.en: The Reward of Two rak'ahs with the Recitation of Surah Al-Ikhlas 120 Times

text: رِوَايَةِ اِبْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «مَنْ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ خَفِيفَتَيْنِ بِ‍ «قُلْ هُوَ اَللّٰهُ أَحَدٌ»  فِي كُلِّ رَكْعَةٍ سِتِّينَ مَرَّةً اِنْفَتَلَ وَ لَيْسَ بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ذَنْبٌ» .

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1558 - In a narration from Ibn Abi Umayr, from Al-Sadiq (as), He said:
"Whoever prays two light (short) units of prayer (Rak'ahs), reciting 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas) sixty times in each Rak'ah, will finish the prayer without any sins remaining between him and Allah (swt), the Mighty and Exalted."
